metadata.title: cho.sh
metadata.description: Coscientist-style reading interface for the Extracranial archive.

hero.title:
Read cho.sh
as a living graph
hero.subtitle: Coscientist's interface, Extracranial's archive.
hero.description: Research notes and blog posts, unified under hex-first document IDs.

features.heading: One reading surface for research and essays

dialecticalGraph.title: Hex-First Notes
dialecticalGraph.description: Every document uses its frontmatter slug as the canonical ID, so navigation is stable even when titles change.

rebuttalFirst.title: Co-located Archive
rebuttalFirst.description: Research pages and blog posts live in one note system, with shared previews, backlinks, and pane navigation.

multilingual.title: Locale-Aware Fallback
multilingual.description: English and Korean variants resolve to the same note ID when possible, while missing translations gracefully fall back.

pipeline.heading: Pane-stacked navigation for long-form archives
pipeline.description: Open one note, branch into linked notes, and keep context visible without losing your place.

forWho.heading: For readers who navigate by connections

audiences.scientists: Researchers following associative trails across notes
audiences.engineers: Engineers revisiting old essays without losing context
audiences.analysts: Readers comparing blog posts against research pages
audiences.teams: Anyone who prefers linked panes over linear archives

cta.heading:
The archive already exists.
This app changes how it is read.
